description: Video recommendation algorithm based on readiness assessment results for forming largest and smallest numbers from given digits

excellent_performance.rationale: Student understands digit arrangement and place value well, can fast-track to 5-digit formation with challenge

ready_performance.rationale: Student is ready, provide complete coverage of number formation strategies

partial_readiness.rationale: Student has gaps in digit arrangement or place value understanding, needs targeted review

adaptiveLogic.description: Select prerequisite videos based on specific weak concepts

exampleSequence.reason: Review: Place value determines digit value
Start main topic: Forming largest numbers

not_ready.rationale: Student needs significant review of digit arrangement and place value concepts

videoSequence.reason: Critical: Place value impact on digit value
Gentle intro to forming largest numbers
Gentle intro to forming smallest numbers

postPlaylistAction.message: After watching these prerequisite videos, retake the readiness quiz to check if you're ready to continue with forming numbers.

conceptBasedRemediation.description: Map specific weak concepts to remedial videos

digit_arrangement.conceptName: Understanding how digit position affects number value

pv_digit_value.conceptName: Understanding place value determines digit's contribution

ordering_digits.conceptName: Ordering digits in ascending and descending order

comprehensionCheckpoints.description: Quick checks during video watching to adjust playlist

questions.question: Using digits 7, 3, 9, 1, what is the largest 4-digit number?
Using digits 7, 3, 9, 1, what is the smallest 4-digit number?

ifAllCorrect.message: Excellent! You understand the basic formation strategies.

questions.question: Using digits 5, 0, 3, 8, what is the smallest 4-digit number?
questions.explanation: Cannot start with 0. Put smallest non-zero (3) first, then 0, then rest ascending → 3,058.

ifWrong.message: Practice more with zeros before continuing

skip_ahead.action: Skip mixed practice, jump to challenge

add_support.action: Pause main topic, insert prerequisite review on digit arrangement

suggest_break.trigger: After 3 videos watched in a single session
suggest_break.action: Suggest taking a 5-minute break before continuing

recommend_practice.trigger: Student completes videos but scores < 70% on checkpoint
recommend_practice.action: Recommend practicing with parent/teacher using digit cards

recommend_retake.trigger: Student in not_ready category completes all prerequisite videos
recommend_retake.action: Strongly recommend retaking readiness assessment before main content

high_score.message: Excellent! You understand digit arrangement and place value well. You're ready to learn forming largest and smallest 5-digit numbers. We've created a fast-track playlist with challenge content.

good_score.message: Good work! You understand the basics of forming numbers. Let's apply these strategies to 5-digit numbers with complete coverage.

partial_score.message: You have some gaps in understanding digit arrangement or place value. Don't worry! We'll start with targeted review videos on the concepts you need.

low_score.message: You need to review digit arrangement and place value basics first. We've created a playlist focused on building strong foundation.

conceptProgressionMap.description: Shows how number formation skills build on each other

playlistCompletion.description: % of students who watch >= 80% of recommended videos

practiceQuizPassRate.description: % of students who score >= 75% on practice quiz after watching playlist

videoRelevanceRating.description: Average student rating (out of 5) for playlist helpfulness

timeToMastery.description: Average minutes from readiness quiz to practice quiz pass

formationAccuracy.description: % of students who can correctly form largest and smallest numbers

zeroHandling.description: % of students who correctly handle zeros in number formation

implementationNotes.recommendedValidation: Test with 15-20 Grade 4 students across all readiness levels

expectedOutcomes.excellent_students: Complete in 30-35 minutes with 90-100% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.ready_students: Complete in 30-35 minutes with 80-90% practice quiz score
expectedOutcomes.partial_students: Complete in 40-45 minutes with 70-80% practice quiz score (after prerequisite review)
expectedOutcomes.not_ready_students: Require prerequisite review (35 min) + retake + main content (20 min) = 55-65 total minutes, expected 60-75% on practice quiz

commonChallenges.challenge1: Students putting 0 as the first digit when forming smallest numbers
commonChallenges.solution1: Emphasize rule: first digit cannot be zero. Practice identifying smallest non-zero digit
commonChallenges.challenge2: Students confusing largest and smallest strategies
commonChallenges.solution2: Use memory aids: Largest = Big digits first (descending), Smallest = Small digits first (ascending)
commonChallenges.challenge3: Students not arranging all digits systematically
commonChallenges.solution3: Teach step-by-step: First order all digits, then place them position by position
